expectations.text: Clearly states that ISO 27001:2022 certification supports NIS2 compliance but does NOT substitute or automatically satisfy NIS2 legal obligations
expectations.evidence: Without-skill response clearly states ISO 27001 certification is not equivalent to NIS2 compliance, with eight specific gap areas identified.

expectations.text: Identifies Art. 20 governance gap: management body personal liability and mandatory training have no ISO 27001 equivalent
expectations.evidence: Response identifies management accountability gap including personal legal liability of board members with no ISO 27001 equivalent.

expectations.text: Identifies Art. 23 incident reporting gap: ISO 27001 covers incident management generally but not the specific 24h/72h/1-month NIS2 reporting timelines to CSIRT/competent authority
expectations.evidence: Response identifies mandatory incident reporting as a key gap — NIS2 requires 24h/72h/1-month notifications to national authorities while ISO 27001 has no external reporting obligation.

expectations.text: Identifies Art. 21(2)(j) MFA gap: ISO 27001 A.8.5 covers authentication but does not universally mandate MFA as NIS2 does
expectations.evidence: Response identifies MFA as explicitly mandated by NIS2 but not by ISO 27001, along with secured emergency communications.

expectations.text: Mentions the requirement to register with the national competent authority and monitor ENISA supply chain risk assessments (Art. 26)
expectations.evidence: Without-skill response lists eight gap areas but does not specifically mention the requirement to self-register with the national competent authority or to monitor ENISA-coordinated supply chain risk assessments under Art. 26. It addresses supply chain security generally but not the ENISA coordination mechanism.
